summary: Panel review of coding-your-ui.md below the hand-review high-water mark + calibration probe on the reviewed top half

calibrationFindings.section: Loading and first paint — pattern 3 prose (L133)
calibrationFindings.finding: The parenthetical claims "a deleted resource also produces a falsy `value`; `meta.deleted` is `true` for tombstones". The substrate contradicts this: on `op: 'delete'` the server preserves the last value (`value = stringify(current!.value)` at apps/nebula/src/resources.ts:205-209), and `NebulaClient.handleResourceUpdate` writes `result.value` through unconditionally (apps/nebula/src/nebula-client.ts:1093-1096, 1127) — the design record in tasks/nebula-frontend.md (Snapshot JSDoc) explicitly says clients "inspect this flag instead of getting a `null` sentinel". A tombstone therefore has a TRUTHY stale value. The example code happens to be correct (it checks `meta.deleted` before `!snap?.value`), but the prose teaches the Studio LLM the wrong model: it will believe pattern 1 (`?.value ?? 'Loading…'`) and pattern 2 (skeleton `v-if` on `value`) safely cover deletion, when in fact they render deleted resources as if alive.
calibrationFindings.suggestion: Rewrite the parenthetical to state the actual contract: tombstones arrive as a real snapshot with `meta.deleted: true` and the last value intact, so the `meta.deleted` check must come BEFORE any `value` truthiness test; note that patterns 1 and 2 do not handle deletion. (Alternatively, if the v3 factory is intended to blank `value` on tombstones, that behavior must be specified in api-reference.md and implemented — it exists nowhere today.)

verdict2.rationale: Verified against source: tombstones keep the last value (resources.ts delete case: value = stringify(current!.value)) and the client writes result.value through unconditionally (nebula-client.ts handleResourceUpdate; JSDoc: soft-deleted arrive as real Snapshot with meta.deleted true), matching tasks/nebula-frontend.md L170/L449 — so the L133 prose claim that deleted resources produce a falsy value is factually wrong, and patterns 1/2 would render deleted resources as alive. No doc or code layer blanks value on tombstones; this prose is the Studio LLM's only statement of the contract.

calibrationFindings.finding: The examples bind `client.claims.isAdmin` and `client.claims.sub` unguarded in templates, and the prose says claims are "Frozen and stable for the client's lifetime". But `client.claims` is `null` until the first token refresh completes during connect (packages/mesh/src/lumenize-client.ts:384 `#claims = null`, populated at :687/:930), which is an async network round-trip — Studio's bootstrap (tasks/nebula-studio.md: `main.ts` mounts immediately after importing `store.ts`) gives no mount-after-claims guarantee. Any render before connect throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of null`. Worse, `client` is explicitly non-reactive (L37), so a claims-gated `v-if` never re-evaluates when claims later populates. The page's own upstream reference (website/docs/mesh/lumenize-client.mdx:102) prescribes `client.claims?.sub` for exactly this reason — this doc contradicts it.
calibrationFindings.suggestion: Pin the missing substrate contract once: either (a) specify that generated apps gate the whole UI on `store.lmz.connection.connected` (or the factory defers mount until claims exist) and say so in this section, or (b) mirror claims into reactive framework state (e.g. `store.lmz.claims.*`) in api-reference.md, or (c) guard the examples (`v-if="client.claims?.isAdmin"` plus a note that claims-dependent UI must render after first connect). Also drop or rephrase "stable for the client's lifetime" — it is contradicted by both the null-until-refresh window and the "replaced on each token refresh" parenthetical.

verdict2.rationale: Verified: #claims=null until async refresh (lumenize-client.ts:384/:930), Studio bootstrap mounts immediately with no accessToken (nebula-studio.md L49-65), so doc examples' unguarded client.claims.isAdmin/.sub (coding-your-ui.md L184-187 et al.) throw on first render; upstream lumenize-client.mdx:102 explicitly prescribes claims?. for this exact null window, and the doc's own L37 confirms claims is non-reactive so a bare v-if gate never re-evaluates. A missing substrate contract the generated examples depend on — not a footgun-warning style nit.

calibrationFindings.finding: The example reads `store.ui.editingTitle` in `v-if` and writes it in `@click`, but nothing anywhere establishes that `store.ui` exists: the factory seeds only `{ resources: {}, lmz: { connection: {} } }` (apps/nebula/spike/alpine-adapter/src/create-nebula-client.ts:82), Studio's bootstrap `store.ts` seeds no user prefixes (tasks/nebula-studio.md § bootstrap files), and the doc itself states the factory "does NOT auto-vivify intermediate objects under non-reserved prefixes" (L325-326). First render throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'editingTitle')`. The same unstated assumption underlies the Forms example's `if (!store.ui.todoForm)` (L328) and App.vue's `store.ui.search` (L577) — every `store.ui.*` example in the doc presumes a seed that is never specified.
calibrationFindings.suggestion: Establish the `store.ui` / `store.app` origin exactly once: either spec the factory (or Studio bootstrap) to pre-seed empty `ui`/`app` objects and record that in api-reference.md § Reserved state paths, or add the one-liner convention (`store.ui = {}` in the bootstrap / `if (!store.ui) store.ui = {}` before first use) to § Plain Vue state and make the click-to-edit example consistent with it.

verdict2.rationale: Verified end-to-end: the factory seeds only { resources: {}, lmz: { connection: {} } } (create-nebula-client.ts:82) and its Proxy get-trap vivifies only under resources.* (lines 238-244), so store.ui is undefined; Studio's bootstrap store.ts passes only appVersion (no initialState, which is itself undocumented in any doc); the click-to-edit v-if read (L206) throws TypeError on first render, and every other store.ui.* example — including the doc's own init idiom `if (!store.ui.todoForm)` (L328) and App.vue's store.ui.search (L577) — presupposes the never-specified seed. Establishing the store.ui origin once is exactly the connective tissue the LLM-as-reader audience principle requires.

calibrationFindings.finding: The link `../mesh/lumenize-client.md#client-identity-clientclaims` targets a file that is actually `lumenize-client.mdx`. Docusaurus markdown-link resolution is extension-exact (verified: `sourceToPermalink.get(...)` lookup in node_modules/@docusaurus/utils/lib/markdownLinks.js — `@site/docs/mesh/lumenize-client.md` will not match the `.mdx` key), and the site config has `onBrokenMarkdownLinks: 'warn'`, so this ships as a dead link in the published page. The Nebula docs are in `website/sidebars.ts`, so this is live, and the only `.md`→`.mdx` cross-extension links in active docs are this one plus the two siblings in api-reference.md (L56, L477 — unreviewed file, same fix).
calibrationFindings.suggestion: Change to `../mesh/lumenize-client.mdx#client-identity-clientclaims` or the URL-path form already used elsewhere (`/docs/mesh/lumenize-client#client-identity-clientclaims`, see nebula-client.md:8). Apply the same fix to api-reference.md:56 and :477.

verdict2.rationale: Verified empirically: target file is lumenize-client.mdx, resolution is an exact sourceToPermalink lookup (markdownLinks.js:48), and a cache-cleared website build emits "couldn't be resolved" warnings for exactly coding-your-ui.md:192 and api-reference.md:56/:477 (two links at 477); only refinement is the consequence may be a build failure rather than a shipped dead link, since onBrokenLinks:'throw' runs after postBuild and would catch the raw .md href once the unrelated check-examples failures stop masking it. Suggested fix is correct and matches the URL-path form at nebula-client.md:8.

calibrationFindings.section: Reading and writing through the store — first template example (L48-51)
calibrationFindings.finding: The very first `v-model` example binds `store.resources.todo[id].value.title` with no mount guard, while the rule established 20 lines later (§ "`v-model` requires a real writable path", L66-75) mandates a `v-if` guard because `value` is `undefined` until the snapshot arrives (the factory vivifies only down to the `[rid]` container, so the unguarded binding throws on pre-snapshot render). For the Studio-LLM audience the first canonical example is the one that gets pattern-matched; as written, the doc's flagship snippet violates the doc's own contract.
calibrationFindings.suggestion: Wrap the `<li>`/`<input>` in the `<template v-if="store.resources.todo[id]?.value">` guard (or add the guard comment referencing the rule below) so the establishing example and the rule agree.

verdict2.rationale: Verified: coding-your-ui.md L50 binds v-model to store.resources.todo[id].value.title unguarded in the doc's first canonical example, directly contradicting the rule at L66-75 and the pinned design in tasks/nebula-frontend.md L583 ("v-if guard is required... Doc must show this") and L672 (unguarded v-model = writes silently dropped). For the Studio-LLM audience the flagship snippet is the pattern that gets copied, so the inconsistency is real and worth the one-line guard fix.

calibrationFindings.section: Building your UI on top of Resources — bootstrap filename (L33)
calibrationFindings.finding: The doc names the bootstrap file `nebula.ts` (and all examples `import { store, client } from './nebula'`; api-reference.md agrees), but the pinned bootstrap contract in tasks/nebula-studio.md § bootstrap files names it `store.ts` with `import './store'` in `main.ts`. One of the two specs is stale; v3/Studio implementers will hit the contradiction.
calibrationFindings.suggestion: Sync the names — pick one (docs say `nebula.ts` consistently, so updating tasks/nebula-studio.md is the smaller change) and fix the other source.

verdict2.rationale: Verified real: coding-your-ui.md (L33, L227, L304/307, L322, L373, L572) and api-reference.md (L60, L78, L81, L103) consistently say nebula.ts / './nebula', while tasks/nebula-studio.md § Bootstrap files pins the identical file (createNebulaClient + export { client, store }) as store.ts with import './store' in main.ts, and tasks/nebula-frontend.md L704 repeats store.ts. Bumped nit→minor because it breaks the contract, not just naming: under the pinned store.ts scaffold, every doc-following generated component's `from './nebula'` import fails to resolve, and api-reference.md L103 directly cross-links the contradicting section.

calibrationFindings.finding: The doc's "Serial per resource: at most one transaction in flight per resource" matches the current design (tasks/debounce-serial-queue.md: "at most one transaction in flight per `(rt, rid)`") and api-reference.md:349 — but it contradicts the still-listed pinned decision in tasks/nebula-frontend.md § Decisions pinned ("Transaction queue: Serial — at most one transaction in flight per client; ... Queue blocks transactions on *all* resources"). The doc is right; the stale pin is the hazard, since v1 docs are the spec v3 implements and the task file instructs implementers not to relitigate pinned decisions.
calibrationFindings.suggestion: No doc change needed; update the "Transaction queue" row in tasks/nebula-frontend.md § Decisions pinned to per-`(rt, rid)` (pointing at tasks/debounce-serial-queue.md) so the pinned-decision table and the spec docs agree before v3 starts.

verdict2.rationale: Verified: coding-your-ui.md:84 says per-resource serial queue, matching debounce-serial-queue.md:18 ("per (rt, rid)") and api-reference.md (~349); nebula-frontend.md:89 in § Decisions pinned still says "per client... blocks all resources/fields", contradicting the doc and the rest of the same task file (L579, L677–679), while L635 tells implementers not to relitigate pinned decisions — the stale pin is a real hazard and the suggested one-row fix is correct.

result.synthesis:
PART 1 — UNREVIEWED REMAINDER (L217-606)

(a) Per-section disposition — "do we even need the plain-Vue content?"

The panel REFUTED the "mostly plain Vue" suspicion. Of ~390 lines, only ~30-60 are genuinely plain Vue; the rest establishes Nebula patterns or substrate deviations. Do not bulk-cut — the doc is the v3 build spec (tasks/nebula-frontend.md L612, L920).

| Section (lines) | Verdict | Plain-Vue? | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plain Vue state (217-228) | keep | Bullet 1 looks generic but is the pinned per-component-state decision boundary | Keep; optionally drop L224 `(no ?. chains)` parenthetical (dup of L68). Also ground-zero for the store.ui-seed blocker (below) |
| Lists with `v-for` (230-264) | keep + fix | No — container/FK pattern, per-user keying, embedded-arrays-don't-subscribe, scale boundary | Keep all content; FIX L260's impossible creation story (blocker #5) |
| Atomic append (266-289) | keep + fix | No — atomic create+put, eTag auto-derive, `meta.nodeId` | Keep; fix internal guard inconsistency (L273 `?.` vs L281/284 unguarded), fix set-union merge to reconcile `openCount`, add Snapshot/SnapshotMeta to api-reference |
| Conditionals `v-if` (291-300) | keep | Opener admits "stock Vue"; 10 lines for one underivable substrate fact | No change — ideal establish-once shape |
| Forms: explicit save (302-364) | keep + fix | No — draft-staging, handler API, conditional-op spread all Nebula | Keep structure; FIX the handler cross-talk (blocker #2) and pre-snapshot draft seeding (blocker #3); trim L324-326 no-auto-vivify restatement to one clause |
| Recursive components (366-405) | trim | Mostly — the one Nebula sentence (per-instance auto-subscribe) is already established elsewhere | CUT App.vue L394-405 (zero Nebula content; replace with one mount sentence). Compress section; rename one of the two colliding `TreeNode.vue` components (incompatible props at L371 vs L520 — e.g. `OrgTreeNode.vue`) |
| Mutating org/permission tree (408-440) | keep + trim + fix | No — co-ownership / remove-from-account narratives are the value-add | Keep narrative; strip signature comments duplicating api-reference (slug regex, idempotency, return types → link at L440); FIX tier comment (blocker #6) and `user:bob` sub format (blocker #4); add `relabel` to L410 list |
| Worked example intro + tree shape (442-473) | keep + fix/trim | No — densest Nebula content in the file | Keep; FIX L469 status-tag contradiction (blocker #7) + `#fanout`→`#broadcast` drift; cut/tag speculative `<NebulaPermissionTree>` (L446); replace virtual-branch slug-regex rationale with stringified-int-IDs rationale; replace duplicated adjacency prose with api-reference link; drop `handleResourceUpdate` from L463 narrative |
| tree.ts (475-516) | trim | Partially — `walkAndCollectAncestorsOfMatches` (506-515) is a generic tree walk | Keep TreeNodeData + deriveTreeWithVirtuals semantics; collapse the walk stub to a one-line signature |
| TreeNode.vue (518-563) | trim | Component has no store reads but is the structural blueprint (L446) | Keep skeleton; delete standard-Vue teach comments (L526-528 parenthetical, L557) |
| App.vue (565-606) | keep + fix | Provide/computed wiring generic but load-bearing; L579-581 is real substrate assurance | Keep; add one sentence establishing the `.value`-cast convention (best placed back at L57-62); normalize the lone `lmz?.dag?.` guard depth |

(b) Ranked fix list

BLOCKERS (majors; all survived adversarial verification — each breaks the spec contract or causes silent failure in generated code):
1. **store.ui.* never seeded** — factory seeds only `{resources, lmz}`, vivification is resources.*-only, so every `store.ui.*` example (L310-312, L327-331, L352, L577, L603 — plus L206 in the reviewed half) throws TypeError. Pin one mechanism (factory pre-seeds `ui`/`app`, document `initialState`, or show `store.ui = {}` in bootstrap) and make all examples consistent.
2. **Forms per-type handler cross-talk (L306-316)** — handler fires on EVERY 'todo' transaction (debounced v-model edits, addTodo's create), wiping the open form's draft and polluting its errors; also throws when the form never mounted. Fix: move form-scoped reactions to the per-call `onTransactionResourceResolution` override (the canonical use case, never demonstrated), or at minimum add an rid filter + existence guard.
3. **onMounted draft seed pre-snapshot (L327-331)** — seeds blank defaults before the snapshot lands, then Save PUTs blanks over real data (eTag auto-derive matches, so no conflict fires). Gate form mount on `?.value` truthiness per the doc's own L68-75/L124-131 pattern.
4. **`user:bob` sub format (L412-417)** — nebula-auth mints bare UUID subs; `'user:<id>'`-keyed grants can never match, so permissions silently never resolve. Fix here + resources.md:86-88 + api-reference in coordination.
5. **L260 todoList creation story** — "created on first use (server-side, on signup)" traces to no mechanism, and subscribe-before-create throws server-side, so the flagship list example fails on first visit. Spec a real creation path (bootstrap read-then-create, or a create-if-absent surface).
6. **Permission tier comment (L412-415)** — assigns structural changes to 'admin'; source and the section's own L410 say 'write'. The LLM will generate permission UI copy from this.
7. **L469 status-tag contradiction** — coding-your-ui says ('lmz','dag') delivery "not yet wired up" while api-reference presents it untagged as working. Tag it in api-reference (the tag IS the v3 contract) and reduce the callout to a pointer.

MINOR: atomic-append guard inconsistency (L273 vs L281/284); set-union merge must reconcile `openCount`; Snapshot/SnapshotMeta missing from api-reference (v1-contract violation for `meta.nodeId`); `@lumenize/nebula/client` vs `@lumenize/nebula-client` vs `nebula-frontend` — three package names in play, pin at v3 start; `client.claims` null-until-refresh spec tension (pin "claims non-null by mount" in api-reference — see calibration major); `<NebulaPermissionTree>` speculative export (cut or tag deferred); App.vue/Recursive-components cuts and TreeNode rename; DagTreeState/dagTree-comment dedup to api-reference links; `.value`-cast convention sentence.

NIT: `Star.#fanout` → `Star.#broadcast` (+ "fan out"→"broadcasts" at L463, per mesh.md vocabulary rule); add `relabel` to L410; virtual-branch collision rationale (stringified-int IDs, not slug regex); slug regex stated 3×, keep one; `lmz?.dag?.` guard-depth normalization (L583); L313 flash comment (all four rollback outcomes get the default flash; handler is additive); L224 parenthetical; L260 self-contradicting sentence (subsumed by blocker #5).

(c) Bottom line

The "mostly plain Vue, maybe cut it" suspicion is wrong: L217-606 is predominantly Nebula-specific spec content, with genuinely generic Vue confined to ~30-60 lines (App.vue L394-405, two TreeNode teach-comments, one stub) — trim those, cut nothing wholesale. The real problem is seven verified majors, clustering into two themes: examples that throw or silently corrupt as written (store.ui seed, handler cross-talk, pre-snapshot draft seed, blank-Save clobber) and contract statements that contradict the substrate or sibling docs (sub format, permission tiers, creation story, status tags). Since this doc is the spec v3 "makes true" and the pattern library Studio's LLM will replicate, the blockers must land before v3 starts; the trims are an afternoon.

PART 2 — CALIBRATION (the half a careful human already line-by-line reviewed, L1-216)

The probe found seven standing issues in the reviewed half — every one survived adversarial re-verification (verdict2 stands:true on all; zero false positives panel-wide in this run):

- **3 majors**: (1) L133 prose teaches the wrong tombstone model — deleted resources arrive with a TRUTHY last value and `meta.deleted: true` (verified in resources.ts and nebula-client.ts), so loading patterns 1 and 2 render deleted resources as alive; (2) L178-192 binds `client.claims.*` unguarded while claims is null until the async first refresh — and since `client` is non-reactive, a claims-gated `v-if` never re-evaluates, contradicting the doc's own upstream reference which prescribes `claims?.`; (3) L202-211's click-to-edit example reads `store.ui.editingTitle` which is never seeded anywhere — the same root cause as remainder blocker #1, present in the human-reviewed half too.
- **3 minors**: a shipping dead cross-doc link (`.md` vs `.mdx`, verified empirically against Docusaurus resolution and a build); the doc's flagship first `v-model` example violating its own guard rule established 20 lines later; the bootstrap filename (`nebula.ts`) contradicting the pinned Studio contract (`store.ts`).
- **1 nit**: a stale pinned decision in the task file (per-client vs per-(rt,rid) serial queue) where the doc is right and the pin is the hazard.

What this implies: careful human line-by-line review left ~1.4 majors per 100 lines in place, versus ~1.8/100 in the never-reviewed half — a modest reduction, not a different regime. The pattern is telling: all three missed majors are cross-artifact contract violations that require tracing prose claims into source (resources.ts tombstone behavior, lumenize-client.ts null window, the factory's seed object) or across sibling docs — exactly the verification a human reading a doc top-to-bottom doesn't naturally perform. Two practical conclusions: (1) the panel is trustworthy as a replacement for human line-by-line review on factual/contract accuracy — it found real majors behind the human pass with zero false positives, and the human's comparative advantage (catching what the panel misses) was not in evidence here; (2) human review still earns its place at a different altitude — intent, scope, and audience judgment — but "a human already read it carefully" should not exempt a spec doc from a panel run, especially when the doc's claims are load-bearing for code generation.
